executiveSummary: The implementation fully satisfies the PRD for iteration 000002. All 10 functional requirements and all 6 user stories comply. The project is a well-structured Spring Boot 3.4.3 / Java 21 template that enforces hexagonal architecture with vertical slices, uses MapStruct exclusively for DTO/entity mapping, includes a fully working CRUD REST API for SampleEntity, validates inputs with Jakarta Bean Validation, and handles errors through a global @RestControllerAdvice. A README.md documents the architecture and slice structure, satisfying US-004-AC03. A previous compliance report (now superseded) contained errors: it incorrectly flagged FR-1 as partial (PRD says '3.x' not '4'), misread US-003-AC01 as requiring top-level packages (AC01 explicitly says 'nested within the feature directory'), and was written before README.md was committed. Four minor observations are noted but do not affect compliance.

verificationByFR.notes: PRD states 'Java 21 and Spring Boot 3.x (latest stable)'. pom.xml declares java.version=21 and Spring Boot 3.4.3 (latest stable 3.x).
pom.xml is Maven. No Gradle files are present.
spring-boot-starter-data-jpa + H2 runtime dependency declared. application.properties configures jdbc:h2:mem:templatedb with DB_CLOSE_DELAY=-1 and H2 console at /h2-console.

Two @Mapper interfaces present: SampleEntityWebMapper (infrastructure/web) and SampleEntityPersistenceMapper (infrastructure/persistence). No manual field-by-field mapping found in any service or controller.
POST→201, GET→200, GET/{id}→200 or 404, PUT→200, DELETE→204. Status codes are correct per HTTP semantics.

spring-boot-starter-validation declared. SampleEntityRequest uses @NotBlank. @Valid on POST and PUT controller params. GlobalExceptionHandler returns 400 for MethodArgumentNotValidException with per-field messages.
SampleEntity.java (domain) has zero imports. SampleEntityService and SampleEntityRepository (application) import only domain types and java.util — no infrastructure imports.
sampleentity/ is a self-contained vertical slice containing domain/, application/, and infrastructure/ with all relevant classes.
All dependencies are embedded. H2 in-memory. No external services required.
@RestControllerAdvice GlobalExceptionHandler handles SampleEntityNotFoundException (→404) and MethodArgumentNotValidException (→400) with a structured ErrorResponse body.

verificationByUS.notes: AC01: Maven build configured, application starts cleanly. AC02: H2 in-memory auto-configured in application.properties. AC03: server.port=8080. AC04: mvn clean install runs Spotless + compiler + tests — build succeeds.

AC01: POST→201. AC02: GET/{id}→200/404. AC03: GET→200. AC04: PUT→200. AC05: DELETE→204. AC06: README includes curl examples. AC07: DB_CLOSE_DELAY=-1 ensures in-session persistence. AC08: H2 console enabled at /h2-console for visual verification.

AC01: sampleentity/ contains domain/, application/, infrastructure/ sub-packages exactly as specified ('nested within the feature directory'). AC02: SampleEntity.java has zero imports. AC03: SampleEntityService/Repository have no infrastructure imports. AC04: SampleEntityRepository port is in application; SampleEntityPersistenceAdapter adapter is in infrastructure. AC05: Structure is reviewable. AC06: Build passes.

AC01: sampleentity/ contains controller, service (use case), domain model, and repository. AC02: Adding a new feature requires only a new slice directory per README instructions. AC03: README.md documents the slice structure with a visual tree and step-by-step guide for adding new features. AC04: Build passes.

AC01: mapstruct 1.6.3 declared in pom.xml. AC02: Two @Mapper interfaces exist. AC03: No manual mapping in service or controller. AC04: mapstruct-processor in annotationProcessorPaths ensures compile-time generation. AC05: Build passes.

AC01: @NotBlank on SampleEntityRequest.name. AC02: POST/PUT with invalid fields return 400. AC03: ErrorResponse body contains field-level messages joined as a string. AC04: 200/201 are not returned for invalid input. AC05: Build passes.

minorObservations: GlobalExceptionHandler (shared package) imports SampleEntityNotFoundException directly from a feature-specific application package. In strict hexagonal architecture, shared infrastructure should depend on a base exception type (e.g., a base DomainNotFoundException in shared), not on individual feature exceptions. This creates coupling from shared to the sampleentity feature.

SampleEntityService (application layer) carries the Spring @Service annotation, introducing a framework dependency in the application layer. Strict hexagonal architecture keeps application layer framework-agnostic. This is a common Spring convention and not prohibited by the PRD, but worth noting for teams targeting maximum portability.

SampleEntityRequest only validates the 'name' field (@NotBlank). The 'description' field is unconstrained. As a template reference, adding at least one additional constraint (e.g., @Size) on description would better showcase Bean Validation capabilities.

Progress file marks US-003 through US-006 as 'pending' (attempt_count=0) despite the code fully implementing all four user stories. The progress tracking artifact is out of sync with the actual implementation state.
conclusionsAndRecommendations: The prototype is production-quality for a starter template. All FRs and USs are fully met. Recommended improvements (non-blocking): (1) Introduce a base exception class (e.g., DomainEntityNotFoundException) in the shared package so GlobalExceptionHandler does not depend directly on feature-specific exceptions — this prevents shared infrastructure from accumulating per-feature imports as the template grows. (2) Optionally add a @Size constraint on SampleEntityRequest.description to make the validation reference more illustrative. (3) Update the progress file to reflect the completed state of US-003 through US-006. (4) Consider adding at least one integration test (e.g., using @SpringBootTest) to provide regression coverage for the CRUD lifecycle.
